streams.pump_role: single feed containing all reagents pre-mixed in DES
streams.reasoning: From Chemistry Plan stream_logic: All reagents pre-mixed in single DES solution. No incompatibility at room temperature - nitrile oxide only generated at 120°C. Single stream avoids difficulty of metering viscous DES feeds. Concentration adjusted from batch 0.5 M to 0.067 M based on calculation results for reactor sizing.

mixing_order_reasoning: From Chemistry Plan: Single stream design eliminates need for mixer. All components pre-dissolved in DES before entering heated reactor. Nitrile oxide intermediate only forms at 120°C, so premixing is safe and ensures dipolarophile immediately available to trap reactive intermediate.
pre_reactor_steps: Heat feed line to 40-50°C to reduce DES viscosity for reliable pumping
post_reactor_steps: Cool product stream to room temperature
Collect for offline workup with water extraction
chemistry_notes: Thermal 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition driven by in-situ nitrile oxide generation at 120°C. Key design decisions: (1) Single stream avoids viscous DES mixing challenges, (2) BPR required to prevent phenylacetylene vaporization and maintain liquid phase, (3) Calculation shows mass-transfer limitation (Da=39) but acceptable for this reaction type, (4) Short residence time (0.8 min) prevents nitrile oxide dimerization, (5) No light source needed - purely thermal mechanism.

reasoning_per_field.residence_time_min: Calculation estimated 0.8 min (range 0.5-2.2 min). Analogy 1 used 5 min for photocycloaddition but different mechanism. Analogy 3 used 60 min for nucleophilic ring-opening at lower temperature. Using calculation result 0.8 min as it matches batch kinetics with 20x intensification factor.
reasoning_per_field.flow_rate_mL_min: Calculation result: 0.5 mL/min total flow rate. Analogy 1 used 6 mL/min but higher concentration. Analogy 3 used 0.5 mL/min which matches our calculation exactly.
reasoning_per_field.temperature_C: Same as batch (120°C) - Chemistry Plan specifies this temperature required for nitrile oxide generation. All analogies used different temperatures for different mechanisms.
reasoning_per_field.concentration_M: Calculation adjusted from batch 0.5 M to 0.067 M for optimal reactor sizing. Analogy 1 used 0.5 M but different solvent system.
reasoning_per_field.BPR_bar: Calculation required 5.0 bar - phenylacetylene bp 142°C close to operating temperature 120°C. Analogy 1 used 34 bar for 200°C operation. Analogy 2 used 47.6 bar for 154°C. Our 5 bar is conservative for 120°C.
reasoning_per_field.reactor_type: Chemistry Plan specifies coil reactor for thermal reaction. All analogies used coil/tubular reactors.
reasoning_per_field.tubing_material: PFA chosen for high temperature (120°C) compatibility and chemical resistance to DES. Analogy 1 used steel for 200°C but PFA sufficient for 120°C.
reasoning_per_field.tubing_ID_mm: Calculation used 1.0 mm. Analogy 3 used 1.2 mm for similar flow rate. 1.0 mm provides good heat transfer at 120°C.
reasoning_per_field.reactor_volume_mL: Calculation: V = τ × Q = 0.8 min × 0.5 mL/min = 0.4 mL. Analogy 1 used 30 mL but much higher flow rate.

literature_analogies: Analogy 2: oxadiazole synthesis at high temperature
